etymology.description: semantic 肉(肉) (ròu, "flesh") + phonetic 夜(夜) (yè) -> armpit
etymology.interpretation: The character 腋(yè, "armpit") uses the component 肉(肉) (ròu, "flesh") to signify its association with the body. The phonetic part 夜(夜) (yè) provides the sound. The armpit, being a part of the body, is logically linked to the reference to flesh.

examples.chinese: 他感到他的腋下出汗(他感到他的腋下出汗)。
examples.pinyin: Tā gǎndào tā de yèxià chū hàn.

examples.chinese: 夏天的时候(夏天的時候)，我经常使用止汗剂在腋下(我經常使用止汗劑在腋下)。
examples.pinyin: Xiàtiān de shíhòu, wǒ jīngcháng shǐyòng zhǐhànjì zài yèxià.
examples.english: During the summer, I often use deodorant on my armpits.

examples.chinese: 这个体操动作需要抬高手臂，保持腋下的紧张(這個體操動作需要抬高手臂，保持腋下的緊張)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège tǐcāo dòngzuò xūyào tái gāo shǒubì, bǎochí yèxià de jǐnzhāng.
examples.english: This gymnastics move requires lifting the arms high, keeping tension in the armpits.
